gitCommit.subject: feat(stock): 10 melhorias de qualidade 10/10 no módulo Estoque (#945)
gitCommit.body:
feat(stock): 10 melhorias de qualidade 10/10 no módulo Estoque (#945)

* perf(stock-filter): memoize normalize() + pre-compute productNormals in StockIndexes

Eliminates O(N×M) redundant string normalization in the hot filter loop:

- Adds module-level _normalizeCache (Map<string,string>): normalize("Azul") is
  computed once (.toLowerCase+NFD+replace+trim) and returned from cache on all
  subsequent calls. For a 1500-product catalog with ~8 color names, hit ratio
  approaches 100% after first filter pass.
- Extends StockIndexes with productNormals (nameN/skuN/categoryN/supplierN per
  productId): built for free during the existing buildStockIndexes() scan.
- Uses productNormals in applyStockFilters() hot loop to skip normalize() calls
  for category/supplier/name/sku per candidate product via matchSearchIdx().
- Removes dead matchSearch() private helper (fully replaced by matchSearchIdx).
- All public exports remain API-compatible — 124 tests pass without modification.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

* security(stockFetcher): add ALLOWED_FILTER_KEYS whitelist for fetchPaginatedFromBridge

Defense-in-depth against arbitrary column injection via the `filters` parameter.
Before this, any key in the Record<string, unknown> filters object was passed
directly to PostgREST .eq()/.in()/.is() without validation.

- Exports ALLOWED_FILTER_KEYS (Record<table, ReadonlySet<column>>) covering the
  three tables currently called with filters in production:
    products         → ['active','is_active','supplier_id','category_id','brand']
    product_variants → ['is_active','product_id','color_id','supplier_id']
    variant_supplier_sources → ['is_active','variant_id','supplier_id']
- Tables absent from the map are unrestricted (categories, suppliers, etc. are
  never called with filters in production and require no guard).
- Unknown columns on guarded tables emit logger.warn and are skipped silently
  so a misconfigured caller degrades gracefully rather than throwing.
- All 32 existing stockFetcher tests pass unchanged.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

* perf(useVariantStock): decouple stockIndexes from alerts + merge 4 facet scans into 1

Improvement #3 — re-render fixes:

1. Remove `productsWithAlerts` from `buildStockIndexes`/`StockIndexes`:
   - `stockIndexes` dependency: [productStocks, alerts] → [productStocks]
   - Dismissing an alert no longer triggers a full O(N) index rebuild
   - `applyStockFilters` builds `alertProductIds` lazily (only when showOnlyWithAlerts: true)

2. Merge 4 separate O(N×M) facet scans into 1 useMemo pass:
   - availableCategories, availableSuppliers, availableColorGroups, allColors
   - Single loop over productStocks + variants, 4× fewer allocations

Updated 10 test files to call buildStockIndexes(products) without the now-removed alerts arg.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

* perf(inventory): substituir paginação por scroll virtual em VariantStockTable

Troca PAGE_SIZE=50 + paginação por useVirtualizer (@tanstack/react-virtual v3),
permitindo 5k+ SKUs sem degradação de render. Apenas a janela visível + overscan=8
são montados no DOM; spacer rows (<tr aria-hidden>) calibram o scrollbar sem
renderizar os itens fora de vista.

Mudanças principais:
- Remove currentPage, totalPages, safePage, pagedRows e controles de paginação
- Adiciona scrollContainerRef + rowVirtualizer (estimateSize ≈ 56px, overscan 8)
- Deep link ?product=ID migrado para rowVirtualizer.scrollToIndex
- useStockSelection e onSelectAll operam sobre allFlatRows (todos os filtrados)
- Status filter / inline search chamam scrollToIndex(0) ao mudar critérios
- TableHeader.sticky ajustado para top-0 dentro do novo scroll container
- PAGE_STORAGE_KEY adicionado ao LEGACY_STORAGE_KEYS para limpeza automática
- Teste de paginação reescrito como contrato de scroll virtual (mock useVirtualizer)

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

* test(stock): add 4 missing edge-case tests to stockFetcher

410 mid-pagination: verifies partial data returned + reportSilentEmpty
called when bridge deprecates after page 1 (not just on first call).

Null-cursor halt: last record with id=null triggers the stuck-cursor
guard (nextCursor === null path), distinct from the same-id-twice case.

5+ suppliers: regression guard for BUG-H — 6 sources across distinct
supplier_ids all accumulated into currentStock (210) and inTransitStock
(105), not just the most-recently-updated source.

nextStockPairs sparse slots: pure-function tests for a source where only
slots 4-6 are populated; verifies buildFutureEntries emits exactly 3
pending entries (id suffixes 4/5/6) and skips null/undefined slots 1-3.

39 tests total, all passing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

* refactor(types): separate UI fields from VariantStockCore in stock types

Extracts presentation-layer fields (imageUrl, colorHex, colorGroup) from
the monolithic VariantStock interface into a dedicated VariantStockUIFields
interface. Business-logic fields (quantities, status, dates) move into
VariantStockCore. VariantStock remains as VariantStockCore & VariantStockUIFields
— structurally identical to the original, so all 49 consumers compile
unchanged (verified: tsc --noEmit returns 0 errors).

Consumers that only need business logic (filters, aggregation, alerts) can
now explicitly accept VariantStockCore to make clear they have no dependency
on the presentation layer.

Also fixes VariantStockTable.flat-only test broken by the virtual-scroll
refactor: adds the same useVirtualizer mock pattern used in pagination-sku
test so all 3 tests render rows correctly in jsdom.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

* feat(stock): graceful degradation for partial table fetch failures

When categories or suppliers fetch fails, the function now catches the
error per-table, logs a warning, and continues with partial data instead
of rejecting the entire Promise.all. degradedTables[] in the return
lets callers surface a partial-data banner to the user.

- stockFetcher: added graceful<T>(table) helper wrapping secondary
  fetches (variant_supplier_sources, categories, suppliers)
- Return type extended with degradedTables: string[]
- useVariantStock: exposes isDegraded + degradedTables from hook return
- Tests: fixed toEqual assertion + added degradation scenario (categories
  failure → products still returned, degradedTables=['categories'])

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

* perf(stock): useDeferredValue for inline search + sessionStorage for UI state

- Input now updates instantly; the expensive SKU filter (potentially
  thousands of rows) runs at React deferred priority, eliminating
  janky renders on rapid keystrokes.
- Scroll container shows opacity-60 while deferred search is in-flight,
  giving immediate visual feedback that filtering is pending.
- readStored/writeStored now use sessionStorage instead of localStorage:
  inline search, status filter and rupture horizon should not survive
  a new browser session. Legacy key purge still targets localStorage
  (those are the old cross-session keys to clean up).

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

* feat(db): add partial keyset pagination indexes for stockFetcher

Three partial indexes on (id ASC) WHERE is_active = true covering the
tables fetched with fetchPaginatedFromBridge under an is_active filter:
products, product_variants, variant_supplier_sources.

Without these, each page request triggers a full sequential scan + sort.
With partial indexes the planner uses a forward index scan starting at
lastId and stops after 1000 rows — O(page_size) instead of O(N).

Tables fetched without is_active filter (categories, suppliers,
product_images, mv_stock_velocity) already use the primary key B-tree
for ORDER BY id, so no additional index is needed there.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

* feat(stock): structured performance logging with phase timing

Adds high-resolution performance.now() timers around the three critical
phases of fetchAndProcessStockData and emits a single structured log
entry at completion:

  [Stock] perf  { fetchMs, processMs, alertsMs, totalMs,
                  products, variants, sources, futureEntries, alerts,
                  degradedTables? }

This lets operators identify in production whether slowness is in the
parallel DB fetch (fetchMs), the O(N×M) mapping loop (processMs), or
the alert generation scan (alertsMs) — without modifying the function
or adding any external dependency.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(stock): 10 melhorias de qualidade 10/10 no módulo Estoque (#945)

* perf(stock-filter): memoize normalize() + pre-compute productNormals in StockIndexes

Eliminates O(N×M) redundant string normalization in the hot filter loop:

- Adds module-level _normalizeCache (Map<string,string>): normalize("Azul") is
  computed once (.toLowerCase+NFD+replace+trim) and returned from cache on all
  subsequent calls. For a 1500-product catalog with ~8 color names, hit ratio
  approaches 100% after first filter pass.
- Extends StockIndexes with productNormals (nameN/skuN/categoryN/supplierN per
  productId): built for free during the existing buildStockIndexes() scan.
- Uses productNormals in applyStockFilters() hot loop to skip normalize() calls
  for category/supplier/name/sku per candidate product via matchSearchIdx().
- Removes dead matchSearch() private helper (fully replaced by matchSearchIdx).
- All public exports remain API-compatible — 124 tests pass without modification.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

* security(stockFetcher): add ALLOWED_FILTER_KEYS whitelist for fetchPaginatedFromBridge

Defense-in-depth against arbitrary column injection via the `filters` parameter.
Before this, any key in the Record<string, unknown> filters object was passed
directly to PostgREST .eq()/.in()/.is() without validation.

- Exports ALLOWED_FILTER_KEYS (Record<table, ReadonlySet<column>>) covering the
  three tables currently called with filters in production:
    products         → ['active','is_active','supplier_id','category_id','brand']
    product_variants → ['is_active','product_id','color_id','supplier_id']
    variant_supplier_sources → ['is_active','variant_id','supplier_id']
- Tables absent from the map are unrestricted (categories, suppliers, etc. are
  never called with filters in production and require no guard).
- Unknown columns on guarded tables emit logger.warn and are skipped silently
  so a misconfigured caller degrades gracefully rather than throwing.
- All 32 existing stockFetcher tests pass unchanged.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013WFaBwWiXEBqhQwkKbha38

* perf(useVariantStock): decouple stockIndexes from alerts + merge 4 facet scans into 1

   - `applyStockFilters` builds `alertProductIds` lazily (only when showOnlyWithAlerts: true)

   - availableCategories, availableSuppliers, availableColorGroups, allColors
   - Single loop over productStocks + variants, 4× fewer allocations
